channel.title: q-fin.GN updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: q-fin.GN updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: DatedGPT: Preventing Lookahead Bias in Large Language Models with Time-Aware Pretraining
item.description: arXiv:2603.11838v1 Announce Type: cross Abstract: In financial backtesting, large language models pretrained on internet-scale data risk introducing lookahead bias that undermines their forecasting validity, as they may have already seen the true outcome during training. To address this, we present DatedGPT, a family of twelve 1.3B-parameter language models, each trained from scratch on approximately 100 billion tokens of temporally partitioned data with strict annual cutoffs spanning 2013 to 2024. We further enhance each model with instruction fine-tuning on both general-domain and finance-specific datasets curated to respect the same temporal boundaries. Perplexity-based probing confirms that each model's knowledge is effectively bounded by its data cutoff year, while evaluation on standard benchmarks shows competitive performance with existing models of similar scale. We provide an interactive web demo that allows users to query and compare responses from models across different cutoff years.
item.dc:creator: Yutong Yan, Raphael Tang, Zhenyu Gao, Wenxi Jiang, Yao Lu

item.title: Solution to the Equity Premium Puzzle with Time-Varying Variables
item.description: arXiv:2508.17996v3 Announce Type: replace Abstract: The article's aim is to provide a solution to the Equity Premium Puzzle with a derived model. The derived model which depends on Consumption Capital Asset Pricing Model gives a solution to the puzzle with the values of coefficient of relative risk aversion around 4.40 by assuming the subjective time discount factors as 0.97, 0.98 and 0.99. These values are found compatible with empirical literature. Moreover, the risk-free asset and equity investors are determined as insufficient risk-loving investors in 1977, which can be considered a type of risk-averse behavior. The risk attitude determination also confirms the validity of the model. Hence, it can be stated that calculated values and risk behavior determination demonstrate the correctness of the derived model because test results are robust.
item.dc:creator: Atilla Aras
